short_summary: The text explicitly distinguishes real versus fake from natural versus artificial and gives an example of an artificial tsunami that is still real.

source_name: Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents
short_summary: The authors argue that if intelligence is achieved artificially it will be real intelligence created artificially rather than fake intelligence.
why_stronger: This directly attacks the collapse of artificial into fake in a high level conceptual domain.

short_summary: Artificial but not fake is tied to authenticity and to openly non real trees that do not pretend to be natural.

short_summary: A laboratory setting is called artificial but not fake because incentives and outcomes remain real.
why_stronger: This is stronger than product prose because it distinguishes constructed setting from deception in a methodological context.

short_summary: The FCC states that AI technologies generating human voices count as artificial or prerecorded voice and that voice cloning artificially simulates a human voice.
why_stronger: This is stronger because it shows artificial can be legally and functionally precise without meaning fake in every case.
